controller.message: Review completed. I do not recommend early stop: the proposal is well aligned with scalar-trispectrum intent, but its novelty claim needs a sharper gap analysis against boostless bootstrap, cosmological optical theorem, Keldysh cutting, and 2026 dispersion papers.

proposer_001.message: The direction is promising and correctly narrows the novelty to full-shape boostless scalar-exchange trispectra reconstructed from cuts, not to the already-known collapsed collider signal. The main weakness is literature positioning: you should explicitly benchmark against arXiv:2103.08649, arXiv:2205.00013, arXiv:2409.07521, arXiv:2512.20720, arXiv:2512.07941, arXiv:2602.03841, and arXiv:2602.05546. Also make the first calculation more operational: define the dispersion variable, subtraction/contact ambiguity, precise cubic operator basis, and which limits fix which polynomial coefficients.

reviewer_benchmark.same_direction_alternative: A stronger same-direction benchmark would be: construct the leading-derivative boostless massive-scalar exchange trispectrum for the full cubic scalar operator basis, beginning with dot-zeta^2 sigma and (partial zeta)^2 sigma, using 2112.03448/Keldysh cutting data as discontinuities and proving equivalence or contrast with partial-energy recursion/Cosmological Optical Theorem methods. The deliverable should include the collapsed nonanalytic signal, a dispersion-reconstructed full s-channel shape, the explicit finite contact ambiguity matched to the 2106.15468 contact basis, heavy-mass EFT matching, and a compact separable or numerical template for phenomenology.
reviewer_benchmark.comparison: The proposer has essentially the right core calculation, but the benchmark is better because it makes the novelty claim falsifiable against existing exchange-recursion and dispersion literature, includes the complete lowest-order boostless cubic basis rather than one ad hoc extension, and specifies the ambiguity-fixing machinery more concretely. The proposer is slightly more feasible because it is narrower, but less well defended as new.

improvement_comments: Add arXiv:2103.08649 as a central comparator: it already computes exchange correlators via partial-energy recursion and fixes them up to contact terms using unitarity and manifest locality, including scalar inflationary trispectra in examples.

Add arXiv:2205.00013 because it classifies boost-breaking massive-exchange scalar bispectra and operator structures. Even if it is about bispectra, its cubic data are exactly what your cut construction will multiply into a trispectrum discontinuity.

Treat arXiv:2512.07941 as a serious overlap risk. It already studies single massive-scalar exchange and the local/nonlocal trispectrum split using Keldysh/open EFT language, so your new object must be the full momentum shape plus systematic contact ambiguity, not signal classification.

Clarify whether the dispersion reconstruction applies directly to massless zeta correlators with derivative EFT vertices, or first to conformally coupled seeds followed by weight shifting. These are not equivalent implementation details.

Define the dispersion variable and subtraction scheme. Say whether the cut is in internal energy, exchanged momentum s=|k1+k2|, partial energy, or a dimensionless collapsed variable, and state what analyticity assumptions are required.

Replace 'finite contact polynomial fixed by known contact basis and soft/flat-space limits' with a concrete list of constraints: total-energy pole/residue, Manifestly Local Test or absence of folded singularities, permutation symmetry, heavy-mass EFT matching, and possibly normalization against explicit in-in in one kinematic slice.

State the minimal first calculation as a reproducible formula target, for example the coefficient and phase of the (k_L/k_S)^(3/2 +/- i mu) terms for dot-zeta^2 sigma, then the first corrected coefficient for (partial_i zeta)^2 sigma.

Do not overclaim about crossing. For boostless derivative vertices, crossing s/t/u may mix numerator structures and contact terms; phrase this as a diagnostic to be verified rather than an expected automatic property.
evidence_checked: ARC MCP paper tools were attempted first for metadata, abstracts, and citation counts, but each returned 'user cancelled MCP tool call'.
arXiv:2112.03448: derives a local/nonlocal classification and cutting rule for cosmological collider signals, submitted 2021-12-07 and revised 2022-07-08. https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.03448
arXiv:1811.00024: derives scalar-exchange four-point bootstrap seeds, spin raising, and weight shifting to massless scalars. https://arxiv.org/abs/1811.00024
arXiv:2106.15468: gives the general scalar contact trispectrum from manifestly local contact interactions without assuming boosts. https://arxiv.org/abs/2106.15468

arXiv:2103.08649: introduces partial-energy recursion for exchange correlators, fixed up to contact terms by unitarity and manifest locality, and applies it to scalar inflationary trispectra. https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.08649
arXiv:2205.00013: classifies boost-breaking single-exchange cosmological-collider scalar bispectra from massive exchange, relevant as cubic input for the cut. https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.00013

arXiv:2409.07521: derives Keldysh r/a cutting rules for equal-time in-in correlators and states that nonlocal collider signals arise from cut propagators under microcausality. https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.07521
arXiv:2512.07941: studies single massive-scalar exchange and the local/nonlocal trispectrum split using open EFT/Keldysh language. https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.07941
arXiv:2512.20720: derives single-cut discontinuity relations for cosmological correlators and includes derivative-interaction extensions. https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.20720

arXiv:2602.03841 and arXiv:2602.05546: both support discontinuity-to-dispersion reconstruction, with the latter explicitly leaving contact-diagram ambiguities. https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.03841 and https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.05546
tool_queries_used: mcp__arc__.get_abstract paper_ids=[2112.03448,2602.03841,2602.05546,2512.07941] -> user cancelled MCP tool call
tool_search query for subagent capability; subagents were not spawned because the exposed tool permits spawning only when the user explicitly asks for sub-agents
